mission.title: Install and Configure Network Service Mesh on Kubernetes
mission.description: Network Service Mesh provides a way to manage and connect network services in a Kubernetes environment. Installing it allows you to leverage its capabilities for service connectivity and management.

steps.title: Create Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for Network Service Mesh.

steps.title: Install Network Service Mesh Components
steps.description: Apply the necessary manifests to install Network Service Mesh.

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description: Check if the Network Service Mesh pods are running successfully.

uninstall.title: Remove Network Service Mesh
uninstall.description: Delete the Network Service Mesh components and namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Network Service Mesh
upgrade.description: To upgrade Network Service Mesh, apply the new version manifests.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Running
troubleshooting.description: If any pods are not in 'Running' state, check the logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quotas
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that your cluster has enough resources (CPU, Memory) to run the pods.

troubleshooting.title: Network Policies
troubleshooting.description: Check if any network policies are blocking communication between the services.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Network Service Mesh will have all pods running in the nsm-system namespace, and you will be able to manage network services effectively. You should also see no errors in the logs of the Network Service Mesh components.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl configured to interact with it.
